chunks.text: In the desert at Giza, in Egypt, three pyramids stand.
The largest, the pyramid of Khufu
has been standing there for four thousand five hundred years.
Its height is one hundred forty - six meters.
It was constructed of about two and a half million block.
At the time, no such enormous building existed anywhere in the world.
How the ancient Egyptians built it is still not fully known.
The strongest theory today is that they carried heavy limestone and built it up using a ramp.
The pyramid was a tomb of the pharaoh and his eternal home.
The Egyptians believed in life after death
and they designed it as a stairway for the king's soul to rise toward the sky.
The silence of four thousand five hundred years still rests quietly in the wind.
The ancient civilization vanished long ago
but its silence still seems to speak something to us.
The wish of the people who sought immortality has been carved into stone
